sourceMovie.title: Live by Night
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Live by Night for the same reason it broke through in 2016: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Ben Affleck keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: American Psycho
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Live by Night clicked for you, American Psycho (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Live by Night?
faq.answer: Try American Psycho, Donnie Brasco, The Talented Mr. Ripley—each shares DNA with Live by Night while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Live by Night?
faq.answer: Live by Night (2016) is best described as Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Live by Night worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
